key_insight: Constrained Hebbian learning reallocates limited synaptic resources into lower-redundancy representations, improving the cost–information trade-off under structural constraints rather than simply maximizing accuracy.
engineering_relevance: For resource-limited neuromorphic and embedded learning systems, this suggests a biologically inspired way to preserve task-relevant function while reducing representational cost and connectivity demand.
method: Fixed audiovisual embeddings from AVE, Kinetics-Sounds, and VGGSound100 were used to isolate downstream associative plasticity.
Hebbian learning was compared against Dense Difference Target Propagation and backpropagation under matched sparsity and architectural constraints.
Representational cost was measured with mutual-information-based metrics derived from the Variational Information Bottleneck.
evidence_signal: Across the benchmark comparisons, Hebbian learning achieved lower task-information cost than sparse BP and DDTP in the main compressed settings, with performance often remaining comparable though sometimes slightly reduced.
watch_item: The gains are primarily in cost-performance trade-offs, so implementation should account for occasional accuracy loss and the dependence on fixed embeddings and constraint settings.
paper_title: Constrained Hebbian Learning Supports Efficient Representational Allocation under Structural Constraints
